rows.requested_evidence: Clarify the theorem-level novelty beyond finite-group equivariance and the role of order, including small or trivial automorphism groups.
rows.draft_answer: We agree that the equivariance condition in Definition 2.2 is finite-group equivariance on the total direct-sum bundle, not naturality with respect to the thin poset category. The submitted paper already states that the order supplies invariant incidence relations, masks, covers, pair-state communication, and sheaf restrictions, while the action groupoid supplies equivariance; the full UAT itself uses a global broadcast. The submitted evidence does not establish a literature-wide theorem-level separation from generic finite-group equivariant UATs, especially when the automorphism group is trivial. We will narrow the framing and present the order-specific content as the structured/local architecture classes, diameter-depth compilation, and sheaf constructions rather than as a different input-output symmetry condition.
commitments: will: clarify that OENN input-output equivariance is finite-group equivariance on the total bundle, while the poset order supplies invariant incidence, masks, covers, pair-state communication, and sheaf restriction structure

rows.requested_evidence: Reconcile the sheaf UAT headline with the actually universal full and pair-state architecture classes.
rows.draft_answer: The exact class covered by the submitted sheaf UAT is the full OENN on total sheaf-valued fibers, which permits global orbital mixing, and, under connectedness, its cover-local source-labeled pair-state compilation with diameter-scale depth. The paper explicitly distinguishes these from ordinary restriction/adjoint-based local sheaf diffusion or message passing, which is not claimed universal. We agree the headline wording is too broad and will narrow it to this sheaf-indexed OENN completion.
commitments: will: narrow the sheaf universality wording to the proved full or source-labeled pair-state OENN completion and distinguish it from conventional local sheaf diffusion

rows.requested_evidence: Separate imported CENN results from submitted results and make the claimed scope self-contained or narrower.
rows.draft_answer: The submission is not self-contained for the broad CENN framework: Definition A.2 delegates the arrow-bundle generators and Theorem A.3 imports the general UAT hypotheses. What is developed in the submitted text is the finite action-groupoid account of completed OENN equivariance and a selected finite-realization statement; the text also limits the claim to approximants chosen inside the OENN primitive class rather than arbitrary CENN scalar gates. We will mark the general CENN material as imported background, separate it from the finite OENN/action-groupoid results developed here, and narrow the general-form wording.
commitments: will: separate the finite action-groupoid OENN correspondence developed in this submission from broader CENN definitions and UAT results imported from prior work, and narrow the general-form wording

rows.requested_evidence: Address the subject overlap, non-like-for-like baseline comparison, missing repeated seeds, uncertainty, and multiplicity control.
rows.draft_answer: We acknowledge that the submitted EMG-IMU evidence does not support the stated claims of substantial outperformance or improved generalization. The author-visible validation catalog identifies a non-like-for-like baseline protocol, subject overlap between training and held-out data, fewer than two recorded seeds, no uncertainty estimate, no multiplicity correction, and limited validation breadth. The paper provides only the result paragraph and table for these claims. We will remove or sharply qualify the outperformance and generalization language; we do not claim a corrected result.
commitments: will: remove or sharply qualify the claims of substantial outperformance and improved generalization because the submitted evidence does not establish them

rows.requested_evidence: Quantify or qualify the memory, depth, rescaling, compute, sample, optimization, and stability costs of local universality.
rows.draft_answer: The submitted theorem is existential and already states its main structural cost: pair-state variables indexed by carrier and source, per-carrier hidden width equal to the total input dimension, diameter-many inter-site propagation layers, and graph-specific walk-count rescaling in the exact broadcast compilation. We acknowledge that the submission provides no parameter, runtime, sample-complexity, optimization, stability, or implementation study. We will make clear that universality does not establish practical efficiency.
commitments: will: state that the pair-state universality result is existential and does not provide parameter, runtime, sample-complexity, optimization, or stability guarantees

rows.requested_evidence: Provide a precise theorem-by-theorem novelty comparison with closest finite-group equivariant work and explain the role of order.
rows.draft_answer: The submitted evidence supports a scope distinction but not a new verified theorem-by-theorem comparison with all closest finite-group or higher-order UATs. The full theorem approximates continuous G-equivariant maps for a finite group acting on a poset-indexed bundle; the order enters through invariant relations, locality, cover propagation, and sheaf structure, not through a stronger equivariance equation. A broader novelty comparison would require new literature research, so we will not manufacture it and will instead narrow the manuscript claims.
commitments: will: clarify that OENN input-output equivariance is finite-group equivariance on the total bundle, while the poset order supplies invariant incidence, masks, covers, pair-state communication, and sheaf restriction structure

rows.requested_evidence: Identify the exact existing sheaf-network class covered by the UAT.
rows.draft_answer: Universality is not proved for conventional local sheaf diffusion alone. It is proved for the full sheaf-indexed OENN and for the source-labeled pair-state cover-local completion under the stated connectedness, depth, and memory conditions. We will make that exact architecture class explicit wherever the sheaf UAT is summarized.
commitments: will: narrow the sheaf universality wording to the proved full or source-labeled pair-state OENN completion and distinguish it from conventional local sheaf diffusion

rows.requested_evidence: Identify submitted versus imported CENN definitions and theorems and provide complete generators and hypotheses.

This identifies the manuscript's dependency boundary, not a verified literature-wide novelty claim. The submitted evidence does not contain complete definitions of all arrow-bundle generators or a self-contained proof of the general CENN UAT, so supplying that formalization or a new proof in rebuttal would exceed the evidence boundary. We will make the imported dependency explicit and confine the claimed contribution accordingly.
commitments: will: separate the finite action-groupoid OENN correspondence developed in this submission from broader CENN definitions and UAT results imported from prior work, and narrow the general-form wording

rows.requested_evidence: Correct claims that exceed the finite-depth propagation lower bound for anonymous or relation message passing.

The pair-state construction is a sufficient universal completion. We will restrict the text to the depth-versus-distance obstruction and remove the unsupported unrestricted-depth and necessity wording.
commitments: will: restrict the anonymous-message-passing claim to the proved depth-versus-distance obstruction and remove unsupported unrestricted-depth necessity wording
